findings.title: SSRF via redirect following in ingestFromUrl
findings.description: assertSafeRemoteUrl validates DNS of original URL blocking private IPs, but fetch() with redirect:'follow' follows HTTP redirects without re-validating the target. Attacker can host public URL that 302-redirects to http://169.254.169.254/ (AWS metadata) or other internal services.
findings.fix: Set redirect:'error' or redirect:'manual' and re-validate each redirect target with assertSafeRemoteUrl

findings.title: attach() doesn't validate media existence before upsert
findings.description: attach() calls prisma.mediaAttachment.upsert directly. If mediaId doesn't exist, Prisma throws P2003 foreign key violation (500 error) instead of clean 404 NotFoundException.
findings.fix: Add findUnique check for media before upsert; throw NotFoundException if missing

findings.title: remove() race condition — unhandled P2025 on concurrent delete
findings.description: Between findUnique (line 196) and delete (line 217), another request can delete the same media. Prisma.delete throws P2025 (RecordNotFound) which is uncaught, returning 500 instead of 404.
findings.fix: Wrap prisma.media.delete in try/catch; convert P2025 to NotFoundException

findings.title: resolveUrlsToMedia processes URLs sequentially with await in for loop
findings.description: Each URL waits for previous DB lookup + optional HTTP download + media creation to complete. With N external URLs, this is N sequential network round-trips. Used heavily by migrateOrphans which can scan thousands of URLs.
findings.fix: Use Promise.all with concurrency limit (p-map or similar, 5-10 concurrency)

findings.title: resolveUrlsToMedia findFirst without orderBy — non-deterministic
findings.description: findFirst for OR[{url}, {filename}] query has no explicit orderBy. When multiple DB rows match (e.g. same filename via dedup counters), which row is returned is undefined.
findings.fix: Add orderBy: { createdAt: 'desc' } to findFirst for deterministic matching
